commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a pre-provider-normalization quarantine for unserializable agent runtime tool schemas, keeps healthy sibling tools available, and adds focused regression coverage for active and bundled tools.

PR surface: Source +105, Tests +224. Total +329 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection plus the added tests give a high-confidence path: a circular tool parameter schema reaches provider normalization before final runtime projection on current main, while the PR proves it is quarantined before session creation.

target.title: fix(agents): quarantine invalid runtime tool schemas
target.body:
## Summary

Pruned the runtime tool-schema quarantine branch down to the assistant-turn failure path.

This adds a pre-normalization quarantine for active runtime tools whose parameter schema cannot be serialized safely before provider normalization. Healthy sibling tools continue into provider normalization, and the existing runtime-compatible projection still filters model/runtime-incompatible schemas before session creation.

Bundled MCP/LSP tools keep allowlist and final effective-tool-policy filtering ahead of quarantine diagnostics, so policy-disabled bundled tools do not produce misleading blocked-tool warnings.

The test fixtures use neutral `fuzzplugin` / `mockplugin` names.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts` passed on head `508d10096cb`: 3 test files, 65 tests, 24.39s.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.test-support.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.test.ts` passed.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/tool-schema-projection.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.test-support.ts` passed.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` passed.
- Added-line scrub for private paths, secret-looking strings, and the originally reported fixture name passed.
- AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` passed on head `508d10096cb` / main `cef86c1748e`: run `run_6fce05e013f2`, lease `cbx_a1bcddfce965` / `brisk-hermit`, provider `aws`, command 3m09s, total 5m32s.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` exited clean after the bundled-policy fix with no accepted/actionable findings.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: A single active tool with a schema that cannot survive provider/runtime schema projection is quarantined before assistant session creation instead of crashing the whole assistant turn.

Real environment tested: Local focused Vitest/format/lint proof through repo wrappers; remote Linux changed gate through AWS Crabbox.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: See Verification above.

Evidence after fix: Focused runner coverage shows session creation receives only healthy sibling tools when an active tool has an unsupported or non-serializable schema. Projection/quarantine unit coverage verifies provider-repairable schemas are preserved and non-serializable schemas are reported. Bundled-tool coverage verifies policy-disabled bad bundled tools do not emit blocked-tool diagnostics.

Observed result after fix: Healthy tools continue; active bad tools are reported through the existing trusted diagnostic/quarantine path; policy-disabled bundled tools stay quiet.

What was not tested: Live provider calls. Manual/overflow/timeout compaction tool normalization is not changed in this PR and should stay as a separate follow-up hardening PR if we want the same quarantine boundary there.
